phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop in a vehicle to give someone a lift
register at a hotel or an airport
stop by the side of the road in a car
let someone get out of a vehicle; fall asleep
go to a train station, etc, to see someone leave
stay at the same point/level as
go back in the opposite direction

head: off the top of your head
head for/towards a place
head over heels (in love)
tour: go on/take a tour of/(a)round somewhere

questions.sentence: Travellers must show their passport when crossing the ...... between Canada and the United States.
The family ...... at a beachfront hotel just a short walk from the harbour.
Once you ...... the city centre, the museum is clearly signposted on your right.
Prices for flights and accommodation rise sharply during the peak tourist ...... .
My colleague ...... me to the airport whenever I have an early morning flight.
Our class is going on a school ...... to Italy next month.
If we don't leave now, we'll ...... the last train home!
The apartment has a stunning ...... of the mountains from the balcony.
Could you ...... me back some local cheese from your trip to Switzerland?
The ...... from Paris to Rome by train is approximately 1,400 kilometres.
I'd strongly recommend you ...... your accommodation well in advance during peak season.
Although she has lived in Tokyo for years, her ...... city is actually Osaka.
She has always dreamed of sailing around the **world / earth** on her own yacht.
The best way to discover a new **territory / area** is to explore it on foot.
A knowledgeable local can **lead / guide** you to places that tourists rarely find.
The bus **fare / fee** has increased again, so I've started cycling to work.
Darwin's famous **voyage / travel** on the Beagle lasted nearly five years.
The gallery charges an entrance **fee / ticket** of five euros per adult.
The Northern Lights are truly one of the most breathtaking natural **looks / sights** in the world.

C.title: Phrasal verbs
C.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: We drove to the station to ...... my sister off before her long journey to Canada.
As the storm clouds gathered, the hikers ...... for the nearest shelter.
Guests are kindly requested to ...... out of their rooms before eleven o'clock.
The coach driver decided to ...... in at a service station so passengers could stretch their legs.
My neighbour ...... her children up from school every afternoon at three.
The house looks empty — I think they've ...... away on holiday.
Sophie was walking so fast that it was difficult to ...... up with her.
We're planning to ...... back from our trip by Sunday evening at the latest.

D.title: Rewrite with phrasal verbs
D.instructions: Rewrite the sentences using the correct phrasal verb.

questions.sentence:
We can leave for the coast as soon as everyone is ready.
We can ...... for the coast as soon as everyone is ready.

Mum unpacked the bags while Dad registered at the front desk.
Mum unpacked the bags while Dad ...... at the front desk.

Could you let me out of the car right in front of the library?
Could you ...... right in front of the library?

We had driven ten kilometres before realising we had left the tickets at home and had to go back.
We had driven ten kilometres before realising we had left the tickets at home and had to .......

Passengers must remain seated until the aircraft leaves the ground.
Passengers must remain seated until the aircraft .......

Be careful on this road — a cyclist was hit by a lorry here last week.
Be careful on this road — a cyclist was ...... by a lorry here last week.

It was hard for the younger children to stay at the same pace as the rest of the group.
It was hard for the younger children to ...... the rest of the group.

E.title: Write one word in each gap
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: The speed ...... on motorways in the UK is 70 miles per hour.
Why don't we ...... the coastal path instead of going through the town centre?
If you book your flights ...... advance, you can often save a significant amount of money.
I can't remember the name of that hotel off the ...... of my head right now.
I ...... my way in the old town and ended up completely on the wrong side of the city.
My boss is on a business ...... in Tokyo and won't be back until Thursday.
Go ...... ahead at the traffic lights and the stadium will be on your right.
On the left-hand ...... of the street, you'll see a historic church dating back to the 1600s.
We plan to ...... sightseeing in the morning and relax on the beach in the afternoon.
She has been much more cautious about driving since she ...... an accident on the motorway.
I could spend hours ...... the sights of a new city and never get bored.
My parents are ...... holiday in Portugal for the next two weeks.
The guide took us on a fascinating tour ...... the ancient ruins in the valley.
She has always dreamt ...... the ancient temples of Japan.
The company is arranging for all delegates ...... at a hotel near the conference centre.
He often regrets ...... more when he had the opportunity.
Most young people are very keen ...... how to drive as soon as they are old enough.
Please write ...... as soon as you arrive safely at your destination.
When you arrive ......, look for the signs to the baggage reclaim area.
Our neighbours have invited us ...... skiing with them over the New Year.

G.title: Error correction (extra words)
G.instructions: Find the extra word in each line. Click it.

questions.sentence: Did you know that Marco is afraid of be driving on motorways?
We had planned it with our friends to visit the Scottish Highlands for a long weekend.
Marco had always wanted to see Edinburgh and dreamed of living in there.
As we set off, Marco appeared being quite tense about the journey ahead.
We reassured him and continued on to our way north.
After a while, I could see so that Marco was starting to relax and enjoy the scenery.
Then the sat-nav announced: 'We regret it to inform you that the road ahead is closed.'
Marco said he was actually relieved because of he didn't like motorways anyway!
So we all turned around and went to home.

H.title: Word formation (TOUR)
H.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: Being a (1) ...... (TOUR) is not as relaxing as most people imagine.
Before you leave home, you have to organise all the (2) ...... (ARRANGE) yourself, and once you arrive, you must follow a detailed (3) ...... (TIME) to fit everything in.

Still, you rarely encounter any (4) ...... (CULTURE) barriers, someone always volunteers to be the group (5) ...... (PHOTOGRAPH), and you quickly find that the local (6) ...... (INHABIT) are usually friendly and welcoming.

I.title: Word formation (sentences)
I.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: The city centre has been so completely rebuilt that it is almost ...... (RECOGNISE).
Demand for air travel ...... (WORLD) has increased dramatically over the past decade.
High-speed rail travel is based on an entirely ...... (DIFFER) technology from that of conventional trains.
Spending time abroad is one of the best ways to ...... (BROAD) your understanding of the world.
There are no ...... (DIRECT) trains from here to the coast, so you will need to change at the junction.
Passengers are advised to collect their luggage promptly after ...... (ARRIVE) at their destination.
The sign by the road shows the ...... (DISTANT) to the next service station.
There was already a long queue of visitors waiting outside the ...... (ENTER) to the art gallery.
